title: Split Lobby.ts god module — view, network transport, Realtime, polling, and validation in one 2217-LOC class
evidence: Single class Lobby (2217 LOC, ~45 methods) fuses at least five distinct concerns: DOM/view rendering (render*, injectStyle, renderCreateForm, renderBrowse, renderWaitingRoom), raw network transport — seven hand-rolled `fetch(${VITE_SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/...)` calls to create_room/join_room/list_rooms/heartbeat/ready_up/update_player/leave_room (lines 839, 1006, 1091, 1475, 1541, 1722, 1768), Realtime channel subscription lifecycle (subscribeWaitingRoom L1376, cleanupWaitingChannel L1809), heartbeat/browse polling timers (startHeartbeat/startBrowsePoll), and business validation (validationError, duplicateColors/Names, parseSettings). The project already has a dedicated networking layer (NetworkClient) yet lobby-phase transport lives in the UI class.
impact: Any change to the room-join wire protocol, Realtime schema, or lobby UI forces edits to the same 2200-line file; the mixed concerns make the network/Realtime code (a trust boundary) hard to test in isolation and invite copy-paste when a second UI surface needs the same POSTs. Highest-churn UI file (21) so accretion is ongoing.
recommendation: Extract a lobby transport/session module (room create/join/browse/heartbeat/ready/leave POSTs + waiting-room Realtime subscription) mirroring the NetworkClient seam, leaving Lobby.ts as view + input wiring. Move validation helpers to a pure module reusable by tests.
acceptance_criteria: Lobby.ts contains no raw fetch() to functions/v1 and no supabase.channel() calls
Lobby-phase edge-function calls and Realtime subscription live in a separately-importable module unit-testable without the DOM
Lobby.ts drops below the god-module LOC threshold or its class is split by concern
